raw_consumer_statement: I had booked a stay at XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX for XXXX XXXX of XXXX. XXXX XXXX cancelled my reservation due to the hurricane. The did not want to issue a refund. They referred to the terms and conditions at the end of the itinerary. They offered rebooking in XXXX. However, I never signed anything agreeing to those terms and they were not emailed to me until after they had already charged my card. I then filed a dispute with Barclay World Mastercard. They denied the dispute saying the merchant didn't violate the terms. I tried calling to rebook the stay at XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX and spoke to XXXX XXXX. He was mad that I had originally disputed the charge and refused to let me rebook the stay. I sent XXXX an email requesting a refund on XX/XX/XXXX since they would not let me rebook. I received no response. I then proceed to try to file a claim in small claims court against XXXX in XXXX XXXX NC. They haven't been able to serve the summons because there is a locked gate no one will answer. I then contacted XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX and filed a formal request to reopen my dispute. I faxed them all documentation to prove what I said was true and also information showing the person running it has a history of dishonest practice and pending court cases and is in fact using an alias name. They sent a response XX/XX/XXXX stating the merchant had not violated terms and it was outside the permitted time frame for the dispute. I called them on XX/XX/XXXX and spoke with XXXX to ask for clarification of how they did not violate if they refuse to let me rebook and how is the timeframe up when I had 1 year to rebook. She just kept repeating what the letter said. I informed her that I would be filing a complaint with the CFPB.
